beats.activity: Wake up, check on seedlings on the balcony, make coffee.
beats.reasoning: Early riser habit; seedlings are thriving and need morning attention. Mild weather makes this a good day to be outside early.

beats.activity: Drive to Kirkland library, taking I-90 W to avoid any transit disruption fallout.
beats.reasoning: Transit strike until 4pm means driving is the only reliable option; early departure avoids surge pricing and rush-hour congestion on alternate routes.

beats.activity: Morning and early afternoon shift at the library: reference desk, shelving, helping patrons navigate digital resources.
beats.reasoning: Standard librarian duties; morning shift is quieter before midday reference requests pick up.

beats.activity: Lunch break in the break room with a sandwich and a book.
beats.reasoning: Hiro values quiet time and routinely eats at his desk; no need to venture out during strike disruption.

beats.activity: Afternoon shift: busy reference desk as commuters work remotely or seek library services due to transit strike; help multiple patrons.
beats.reasoning: Transit strike likely drives increased library usage as people seek refuge or stable workspaces; afternoon shift extends into early evening.

beats.activity: Drive home to Mercer Island, taking I-90 E as transit restrictions are lifting by now.
beats.reasoning: Transit strike ends at 4pm, so by 5:30pm roads are normalizing; budget 45 min for evening cross-town drive to account for residual congestion.

beats.activity: Spend time in the garden: water beds, tend seedlings, enjoy the mild evening light.
beats.reasoning: Partly cloudy and 68F is perfect for outdoor gardening; this is Hiro's core hobby and a chance to decompress after a strike-affected workday.

beats.activity: Prepare and eat dinner, then hand-write letters to friends.
beats.reasoning: Hiro values proper meals and written correspondence; this quiet evening ritual grounds him after a busy day managing strike-related library traffic.

beats.activity: Listen to vinyl records, reorganize a few albums from the collection.
beats.reasoning: Vinyl collecting is a core hobby; this wind-down activity signals the end of the day and prepares him for sleep.

diary:
Woke early to check on the seedlings—they're really taking off now, which felt like a small win before the day even started. The transit strike meant the roads were oddly manageable despite the chaos; I think everyone just stayed home. Kirkland was busier than usual because of it. People were using the library as a refuge, really, and I found myself helping more folks than usual navigate our digital resources. There's something grounding about that—being useful when the city's infrastructure stumbles.

Lunch was quiet. Just me, my sandwich, and a novel I've been meaning to finish. The afternoon desk shift flew by in a blur of questions and print requests. By evening, the strike had lifted and I drove home through that perfect golden light. Spent time in the garden—there's something meditative about watering and tending things. Wrote some letters by hand, which I don't do enough anymore. Vinyl records and organizing albums felt like the perfect way to close out a day that, despite the disruption, felt oddly whole.
thought_process: I chose to drive rather than risk the transit chaos, even though I usually consider myself pretty transit-friendly. The strike reframed my day—instead of seeing it as an inconvenience, I leaned into the library being essential infrastructure. That shifted my mindset from just going through the motions to actually feeling purposeful. My mood was surprisingly steady; the mild weather helped, and having a predictable drive gave me mental space. I intentionally carved out time in the garden and with handwritten letters because the day's disruption made me aware of how I sometimes rush through evenings. The vinyl records weren't planned—just what felt right after dinner. Small rituals matter when the city's rhythms are off.
world_event_prompt: Mild, partly cloudy Thursday, high of 68F. Citywide transit strike: all Metro buses and Link Light Rail suspended until 4pm. Rideshare surge pricing in effect.
